title: Quercus crassilamellata
taxonomy: Quercus crassilamellata is a species of oak tree. It is classified as a member of the family Fagaceae. Within the Fagaceae family, Quercus crassilamellata is further classified as part of the genus Quercus. The genus Quercus is a group of deciduous and evergreen trees commonly known as oaks. Quercus crassilamellata is native to Asia and is known for its unique characteristics.
parts: Quercus crassilamellata, a species of oak native to China, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The medicinal parts of this plant are not well-documented, but it is believed that the leaves, bark, and roots may be used for their medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in treating various health conditions. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential uses of Quercus crassilamellata. Further studies are necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant.
intro: Quercus crassilamellata, also known as the Quercus crassilamellata or the Quaking Oak, is a plant species within the white oak group, characterized by its broad, rounded leaves and distinctive quaking foliage. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Quercus crassilamellata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Quercus crassilamellata is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and may be used to make a tincture or extract for its medicinal benefits.
